direction_head.model: StandardScaler plus multinomial logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
direction_head.representation: mean and population SD over g=2..16 for each of 27 non-depth coordinates; identical to the external-panel depth grid
direction_head.score_interpretation: uncalibrated out-of-distribution score; not a probability or posterior
direction_head.training_selection: all positive canonical replicates at every migration rate

gate_head.model: StandardScaler plus binary logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
gate_head.representation: selected depth rows plus across-depth mean of 27 non-depth coordinates
gate_head.score_interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not the canonical 243-D gate probability
gate_head.training_target: positive migration with rate >=2.5e-4 versus weak positives plus control

guardrail: Ciona supplies a biologically labelled Southampton direction and a source-negative Poole site control on matched backgrounds. Its released VCF is purpose-ascertained and both hotspot scopes are target-selected. Scores are OOD diagnostics, not held-out validation.

external_expectation.background_matching: source Table S2 Fst: Jer-Sth=0.005, Jer-Poo=0.005, Sth-Poo=0.002; main-text Sth/Poo comparisons are nonsignificant
external_expectation.direction_basis: source HMM ancestry tracts and contact-zone analysis establish recent, asymmetric C. robusta ancestry in Southampton C. intestinalis

figure3_window.basis: chromosome-5 interval used for the source Figure 3 ancestry/tree analysis

southampton_exact_interval.basis: pooled Southampton HMM-positive interval reported in source Table S4

external_expectation.expected_forward_direction: CioAB C. robusta (P3) -> Sth12 C. intestinalis (P2)
external_expectation.guardrail: purpose-ascertained VCF; the hotspot scope is additionally target-selected and is a mechanistic stress test, not held-out validation

model_free_scope_comparator.interpretation: negative f4 and a smaller Sth12-CioAB distance support localized excess Southampton sharing; no ancestral allele or defensible Patterson-D outgroup is available

external_expectation.sampling_caveat: Jersey was sampled in 2014; Southampton and Poole in 2012

input_audit.comparison_locus_contract: same ordered four-population callable-site intersection and cap as the matched Ciona positive/negative panel in this scope

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called across every declared three-population panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

source_scope.kind: purpose-ascertained whole released VCF

simulation_feature_shift.reference: StandardScaler fitted to 2,700 positive regen_full summaries on the identical g=2..16 grid

ciona.ascertainment_caveat: the source removed SNPs private to C. robusta as uninformative for the study's C. intestinalis structure/introgression analysis
ciona.ddrad_contract: 51,141 released SNPs derive from 5,599 linked RAD loci

figure3_window.basis: chromosome-5 interval used for the source Figure 3 ancestry/tree analysis

ciona.mapping_caveat: reads were mapped to a C. robusta reference and mapping rates differed by species

southampton_exact_interval.basis: pooled Southampton HMM-positive interval reported in source Table S4
